description: WolfHsmBackend implements SigningBackend with sign(), public_key_der(), and algorithm() all returning SecretError::Unavailable unconditionally (signing_unavailable() helper). The crate then registers this stub via inventory::submit\!(secretx_core::SigningBackendRegistration { name: 'wolfhsm', ... }). secretx::from_signing_uri('secretx:wolfhsm:label') succeeds at construction time, giving the caller an Arc<dyn SigningBackend> that fails on every operation. Other signing backends (aws-kms, local-signing, pkcs11) return a functional implementation. A caller that selects wolfhsm as a signing backend for a signing workflow (e.g. mTLS client cert, JWT signing) silently receives a backend that never signs. This is security-relevant: the caller cannot distinguish 'configured correctly but transiently unavailable' from 'feature not implemented' without reading source. Fix: either unregister wolfhsm from SigningBackendRegistration until signing is implemented, or return a new SecretError::NotImplemented variant so callers can distinguish capability absence from transient failure.
title: wolfhsm registers SigningBackendRegistration but all signing methods return Unavailable
